assets.prompt:
Create an educational micro-story video for school-age Chinese learners.
Style baseline: 10-15 second school-age micro-story clip, gentle camera move, warm realistic lighting, clear staging, simple shot rhythm, vivid everyday setting.
Scene title: 集合地点
Learning goal: 周末出发前，几个同学在群里确认去北京之前在哪里集合。
Real-life setting: 课堂互动 / 小学高年级或初中预备阶段的中文课堂或校园环境
Character relationship: 老师和学生围绕一个具体任务进行交流、观察或回答
Focus characters: 儿、京、客
Focus words: 哪儿、北京
Fixed spoken line: 你们在哪儿集合？
Reference pinyin for production only: 周(zhōu), 末(mò), 出(chū), 发(fā), 前(qián), 几(jǐ), 个(gè), 同(tóng), 学(xué), 在(zài), 里(lǐ), 确(què), 认(rèn), 去(qù), 北(běi), 京(jīng), 之(zhī), 哪(nǎ), 合(gě)
Story direction: Build a school-age learning micro-story instead of a dry teaching clip. Use a believable 课堂互动 in 小学高年级或初中预备阶段的中文课堂或校园环境. Show 老师和学生围绕一个具体任务进行交流、观察或回答. Let 哪儿、北京 and the focus characters 儿、京、客 appear through a tiny real-life event. The story must contain a setup, a small task or question, a response, and a warm ending. Keep the tone suitable for learners around ages 9-13, a little more mature and grounded than preschool material. Make the spoken line feel natural and land clearly on “你们在哪儿集合？”.
Story structure:
- Opening: establish a real place and a clear student activity.
- Middle beat: introduce one small task, question, or discovery connected to the target words.
- Speaking beat: let the character say the fixed line naturally in context.
- Ending: give a warm response or tiny success moment.
Visual direction:
- Keep the action grounded in family life, classroom life, going out, greeting, helping, reading, planning, traveling, or welcoming someone.
- Use clear staging, readable props, gentle motion, and one main action per shot.
- Keep the same characters, costume, and mood consistent with the matching image.
Text and subtitle rules:
- Reference these exact pinyin spellings for production planning and script checking only: 儿 -> ér; 京 -> jīng; 客 -> kè. Keep these exact Hanzi+pinyin pairs in your notes: 儿 ér | 京 jīng | 客 kè.
- Use the fixed spoken line provided in the script. Reference pinyin for production only: 周(zhōu), 末(mò), 出(chū), 发(fā), 前(qián), 几(jǐ), 个(gè), 同(tóng), 学(xué), 在(zài), 里(lǐ), 确(què), 认(rèn), 去(qù), 北(běi), 京(jīng), 之(zhī), 哪(nǎ), 合(gě). Do not generate pinyin subtitles inside the video; the learning page will show the exact teaching pinyin separately.
- If text appears on screen, prefer Hanzi only and avoid auto-generated pinyin subtitles.
- Hanzi writing and pinyin spelling must be absolutely correct; do not invent, simplify, misspell, or place tone marks on the wrong vowel.
Quality bar:
- Avoid flashcard-style pointing at words.
- Avoid empty backgrounds or generic stock actions.
- Make the moment feel believable, warm, story-led, and useful for a child learner.

Create an educational micro-story video for school-age Chinese learners.

Create a storybook-style learning illustration for school-age Chinese learners.
Style baseline: storybook illustration, soft shapes, warm light, expressive characters.
Scene goal: 周末出发前，几个同学在群里确认去北京之前在哪里集合。
Scene type: 课堂互动
Location: 小学高年级或初中预备阶段的中文课堂或校园环境
Focus characters: 儿、京、客
Focus words: 哪儿、北京
Visual direction:
- Use soft shapes, warm light, expressive characters, and an age-appropriate atmosphere for learners around ages 9-13.
- Build a grounded educational scene connected to real Chinese learning moments such as classroom discussion, home routines, reading, greeting, helping, planning, travel, or going out.
- Include meaningful props related to Chinese learning, such as books, chalkboard, toys, flowers, bags, tables, cups, or other scene-appropriate objects.
- Keep the layout clean and stable, suitable for a modern learning page or printable study material.
Composition:
- One clear main action, readable background, strong focal point, and enough empty space for later educational text layout.
- Make the illustration feel polished, warm, and visually consistent with the Fun Hanzi style, without looking like preschool material.
IMPORTANT:
- Do NOT generate any Chinese characters, pinyin, letters, or text in the image.
- Leave empty space where educational text can be added later.
- Hanzi and pinyin planning notes belong in the separate text layout, not inside the image.
- If any visible text is produced by mistake, it must be treated as an error and regenerated.

Create an educational micro-story video for school-age Chinese learners.
Style baseline: 10-15 second school-age micro-story clip, gentle camera move, warm realistic lighting, clear staging, simple shot rhythm, vivid everyday setting.
Scene title: 说清安排
Learning goal: 爸爸接完电话后，和家人一起确认晚上的安排，大家说话很清楚。
Real-life setting: 家庭时刻 / 温暖真实的家庭空间
Character relationship: 学生和家人围绕生活安排、礼貌表达或简单分工进行互动
Focus characters: 电、话、大
Focus words: 电话、大家
Fixed spoken line: 大家在门口说话。
Reference pinyin for production only: dà jiā zài mén kǒu shuō huà。
Story direction: Build a school-age learning micro-story instead of a dry teaching clip. Use a believable 家庭时刻 in 温暖真实的家庭空间. Show 学生和家人围绕生活安排、礼貌表达或简单分工进行互动. Let 电话、大家 and the focus characters 电、话、大 appear through a tiny real-life event. The story must contain a setup, a small task or question, a response, and a warm ending. Keep the tone suitable for learners around ages 9-13, a little more mature and grounded than preschool material. Make the spoken line feel natural and land clearly on “大家在门口说话。”.
Story structure:
- Opening: establish a real place and a clear student activity.
- Middle beat: introduce one small task, question, or discovery connected to the target words.
- Speaking beat: let the character say the fixed line naturally in context.
- Ending: give a warm response or tiny success moment.
Visual direction:
- Keep the action grounded in family life, classroom life, going out, greeting, helping, reading, planning, traveling, or welcoming someone.
- Use clear staging, readable props, gentle motion, and one main action per shot.
- Keep the same characters, costume, and mood consistent with the matching image.
Text and subtitle rules:
- Reference these exact pinyin spellings for production planning and script checking only: 电 -> diàn; 话 -> huà; 大 -> dà. Keep these exact Hanzi+pinyin pairs in your notes: 电 diàn | 话 huà | 大 dà.
- Use the fixed spoken line provided in the script. Reference pinyin for production only: dà jiā zài mén kǒu shuō huà。. Do not generate pinyin subtitles inside the video; the learning page will show the exact teaching pinyin separately.
- If text appears on screen, prefer Hanzi only and avoid auto-generated pinyin subtitles.
- Hanzi writing and pinyin spelling must be absolutely correct; do not invent, simplify, misspell, or place tone marks on the wrong vowel.
Quality bar:
- Avoid flashcard-style pointing at words.
- Avoid empty backgrounds or generic stock actions.
- Make the moment feel believable, warm, story-led, and useful for a child learner.

Focus words: 电影、一点儿
